entries.statement: Develop and maintain enterprise data and AI strategy, including multi-year vision articulation, strategic priority identification, investment prioritization, roadmap construction, and annual strategy refresh.

Design and lead enterprise data and AI transformation programs, including operating model redesign, organizational change management planning, capability development roadmaps, and transformation team leadership.

Build and lead cross-functional transformation teams, establishing shared objectives, working norms, accountability structures across organizational boundaries, and facilitating cross-domain solutions.
Translate business requirements into operational capabilities, working with technical teams to convert strategic requirements into technical solutions and operationalize novel approaches.

Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessment, future-state requirements modeling, AI workforce readiness metrics, gap analysis, and talent acquisition and development strategy formulation.

Evaluate and develop AI workforce structure, resources, and requirements, including competency framework definition, role design, hiring process management, and team composition for technical depth and cross-functional capability balance.

Manage the data and AI investment portfolio, including initiative prioritization, funding allocation, performance tracking against value hypotheses, portfolio rebalancing based on realized outcomes, and resource allocation decisions.

Conduct data and AI due diligence assessments for M&A and investment transactions, including data asset evaluation, technical architecture review, AI risk assessment, key person risk assessment, and integration planning.

Establish and operate data and AI governance committees, including charter development, membership selection, decision authority definition, meeting cadence management, accountability structure design, and oversight operation.

Build organizational data and AI culture through executive sponsorship, success story amplification, recognition programs, visible leadership commitment to data-informed decision-making, and cultural transformation initiatives.

Report to board, audit committee, and executive leadership on data and AI risk posture, program maturity, regulatory compliance status, value realization, and strategic progress against roadmap commitments.

Design and implement AI adoption strategy that aligns with organizational vision, mission, and goals, including change enablement, technology integration, stakeholder engagement, and organizational readiness assessment.

Lead organizational adoption of AI solutions through cultural and organizational change initiatives, identify barriers to adoption, address resistance, motivate organizational participation, and demonstrate business value realization.

Design enterprise data architecture and systems aligned to governance strategy and business objectives, including information architecture design, system design processes, data model development, and technical infrastructure planning.

Establish and operate comprehensive AI risk assessment and monitoring infrastructure, including iterative risk assessment across system lifecycle, post-market monitoring design, quality assurance procedures, and incident response protocols.

Demonstrate data and AI value to executive stakeholders and board leadership, including quantifying business impact, addressing agency challenges, communicating ROI, and translating technical insights into executive communication.

Coordinate long-range strategic planning with internal and external partners, including capability development planning, strategic engagement activities, partnership synchronization, and integrated course-of-action development.

Design and deliver AI risk management training programs for organizational personnel and external partners, including competency requirement definition, training curriculum development, and organizational capability building.
